title: loxostemon stenolobus
intro: Loxostemon stenolobus, also known as the "forest lophostemon," is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. It is a medicinal plant native to the tropical regions of Asia, particularly in China, Malaysia, and the Philippines. The plant's leaves and roots have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. Loxostemon stenolobus is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is also used in traditional remedies such as ointments and poultices.
